package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "encoding/json"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "io"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for upstream_json_decode_same_response_multiple_times: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: One upstream HTTP response body decoded into multiple targets in the same handler.
type CaseUpstreamJsonDecodeSameResponseMultipleTimesDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseUpstreamJsonDecodeSameResponseMultipleTimesModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseUpstreamJsonDecodeSameResponseMultipleTimesRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseUpstreamJsonDecodeSameResponseMultipleTimesModel
}

type CaseUpstreamJsonDecodeSameResponseMultipleTimesService struct {
    repo *CaseUpstreamJsonDecodeSameResponseMultipleTimesRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseUpstreamJsonDecodeSameResponseMultipleTimesAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveUpstreamJsonDecodeSameResponseMultipleTimes(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseUpstreamJsonDecodeSameResponseMultipleTimesDTO) error {
    resp, _ := client.Get(input)
    if resp == nil {
        return nil
    }
    defer resp.Body.Close()

    body, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)

    var summary struct {
        Items []CaseUpstreamJsonDecodeSameResponseMultipleTimesDTO `json:"items"`
    }
    var metadata map[string]any

    _ = json.Unmarshal(body, &summary)
    _ = json.Unmarshal(body, &metadata)
    c.Set("upstream_metadata", metadata)
    return nil
}
